content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 17,
1992
Leonard R.
Locher
City Clerk
4319 East Slauson Avenue
Maywood,
CA
90270
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—92—314
Dear Mr.
Locher:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) .1/
The Comiuission is not able to provide advice on
past conduct.
Therefore,
we are treating your letter as a request
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (3) .2/
OUESTION
What is a filing officer’s duty with respect to notifying
candidates of their obligation to file campaign statements?
CONCLUSION
When a filing officer is aware that a candidate has failed to
file the required campaign statements, the filing officer has a
duty to notify the candidate of his/her filing obligation.
It
appears from your letter of May 13,
1992, addressed to the
Secretary of State, that you did everything necessary to obtain
compliance.
If the committee does not amend its statement,
you
should refer this matter to the district attorney or the
commission for possible enforcement action.
1/Government Code Section 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3) .)
428 J Street, Suite 800
• P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660


Leonard R.
Locher
Our File No.
1—92—314
Page
2
FACTS
Three candidates jointly formed a committee for election to
Maywood city council.
The election was April 14,
1992.
The first
pre—election campaign statement required to be filed in connection
with this election was due March 5,
1992, covering the period
January
1 through February 29,
1992.
You received the committee’s
Form 490 on May 7,
1992.
Your review indicates that the
committee’s identification number was not disclosed on the
committee’s Form 490.
Subsequently you verified that as of May
18,
1992, the committee had not filed its Statement of
Organization,
Form 410, with the Secretary of State’s office.
You
indicate that you have advised the candidates of this filing
requirement.
ANALYS IS
The Act requires that candidates file an original and one
copy of the Statement of Organization
(Form 410) with the
Secretary of State and a copy of the Form 410 with the local
filing officer within 10 days of qualifying as a committee.
(Section 84101.)
Filing officers are required to notify promptly all persons
and known committees who have failed to file a report or
statement.
(Section 81010.)
In addition, Regulation 18110(a) (1)
and
(2) provide:
(a)
It shall be the duty of a filing officer
pursuant to Section 81010(b)
in connection with
the filing of candidate and committee campaign
statements to determine whether required documents
have been filed.
(1)
In order to fulfill his or her duty to
determine whether required documents have been
filed, if the filing officer is aware that a
candidate or committee has an obligation to
file a campaign statement and has failed to do so,
the filing officer shall notify the candidate or
committee of the obligation to file a statement.
(Emphasis added.)
(2)
In determining whether required documents have
been filed, the filing officer shall not be required
to conduct any investigation to determine whether or


Leonard R.
Locher
Our File No.
1—92—314
Page
3
not a committee or candidate has an obligation to file
a campaign statement.
This subsection is not intended
to prevent the filing officer from performing such an
investigation.
Additional filing officer duties include:
——
Supplying the necessary forms and manuals prescribed
by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
--
Reporting apparent violations of the Political Reform
Act to the appropriate agencies.
——
Compiling and maintaining a current log of all reports
and statements filed.
As noted above, notification of non—filers and late filers is one
of the statutory duties of a filing officer.
Failure to file the
required statements places the committee in possible violation of
the provisions of the Act and subject to late fines.
(Section
91013.)
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please
contact me at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting Gral Counsel
By:
Ann Kvasag
Political Reform Consultant

candidates of their obligation to file campaign statements?

commission for possible enforcement action.

Leonard R.
Locher
Our File No.
1—92—314

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is a filing officer’s duty with respect to notifying
candidates of their obligation to file campaign statements?

CONCLUSION: When a filing officer is aware that a candidate has failed to
file the required campaign statements, the filing officer has a
duty to notify the candidate of his/her filing obligation.
It
appears from your letter of May 13,
1992, addressed to the
Secretary of State, that you did everything necessary to obtain
compliance.
If the committee does not amend its statement,
you
should refer this matter to the district attorney or the
commission for possible enforcement action.
1, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3) .)

Leonard R.
Locher
Our File No.
1—92—314
